Banishing Light: Premium first-pick removal in a format that's starved for it — 55.8 GIH on an enchantment means it dodges most black removal, and ALSA 3.2 means it won't table. Goes in every white deck, period.
Beza, the Bounding Spring: Premium first-pick mythic — 62.6 GIH and 9.3 IWD on a 4-drop that floods the board with tokens and gains life. Fits any white deck, pushes you toward GW Rabbits or BW Bats. Auto-pick.
Brave-Kin Duo: Below-rate stats and a -2.8 IWD tell the story — a dual-tribe 1-drop that rarely triggers anything relevant on turn 1. Solid filler only in GW Rabbits or RW Mice when you desperately need a 1-drop curve.
Brightblade Stoat: Strong playable Weasel Soldier — 57.9 GIH and ALSA 2.8 confirm it's contested early. Vigilance plus a Solider/Weasel body glues together multiple white archetypes; take it highly when in white.
Builder's Talent: Archetype-only payoff — the 56.8 GIH inflates when paired with unblockable creatures (Shoreline Looter), but the 51.7% play rate and ALSA 5.2 reveal it often wheels. Build-around for WU Birds or sneaky RW go-wide.
Caretaker's Talent: Strong playable Class enchantment — 58.3 GIH at ALSA 2.6 means it's taken early and delivers. Level 1 gains life; Level 3 reanimates tokens. Best in BW Bats where lifegain is a tribal payoff, solid in any white deck.
Carrot Cake: Solid filler that overperforms — 58.3 GIH on a 2-mana Food that makes a 1/1 Rabbit and scry 1 hits multiple axes. Premium in BW Bats and GW Rabbits; strong enough to play in any white deck as cheap fodder.
Crumb and Get It: Solid filler instant — 55.7 GIH on a 1-mana spell that creates Food and draws cards when Rabbits are out. Roles include enabling Forage and stalling; lower priority outside RW/GW go-wide.
Dawn's Truce: Skip it — 48.7 GIH and -2.2 IWD confirm this conditional peace enchantment underdelivers. A 27% play rate says drafters agree; symmetrical suppression rarely helps the aggressor. Sideboard/replacement-level at best.
Dewdrop Cure: Sideboard-level life gain — 50.4 GIH and -3.7 IWD are brutal for a 3-mana sorcery that heals and bounces. Almost never maindecked (28.1% play rate); only relevant in hyper-aggressive metas where you're on defense.
Driftgloom Coyote: Strong playable at 5 mana — 57.2 GIH with ALSA 3.0 shows it's contested. Flying 3/4 with Offspring makes two bodies and trades up in air; solid top-end for GW or BW skies decks.
Essence Channeler: Strong playable rare — 57.0 GIH with 4.5 IWD and ALSA 2.3 means it goes early. A 2-drop Bat Cleric that grows off your lifegain triggers is a premium engine piece in BW Bats; still solid in any white deck.
Feather of Flight: Solid filler Aura — 57.1 GIH with ALSA 4.5 means it's available mid-pack. Granting flying to a ground creature upgrades attackers in GW or BW; never exciting, always playable. Don't take over creatures.
Flowerfoot Swordmaster: Solid filler 1-drop Mouse — 54.5 GIH and ALSA 4.4 say it's freely available. First strike on the attack is a relevant combat upgrade in RW Mice go-wide; mediocre elsewhere. Fine 23rd card.
Harvestrite Host: Strong playable — 59.0 GIH and ALSA 3.4 mark a 3/3 Rabbit that pumps the board when it ETBs via Gift. Excellent in GW Rabbits; a solid curve-topper in any white wide deck.
Hop to It: Strong playable token spell — 58.8 GIH and a 3.5 IWD confirm three 1/1 Rabbits is exactly what GW Rabbits and RW Mice want. Fills out go-wide plans and triggers lord effects; high pick within those archetypes.
Intrepid Rabbit: Premium white common — 57.9 GIH and ALSA 3.7 on a Rabbit that always generates Offspring value. The 2-for-1 at 3 mana powers GW Rabbits and is strong enough to run in any white go-wide shell. Pick highly.
Jackdaw Savior: Strong playable rare — 56.5 GIH and ALSA 2.2 mean it goes early. A Bird Cleric with lifegain upside and a solid body fits BW Bats and WU Birds naturally; a reliable pick in any white midrange shell.
Jolly Gerbils: Solid filler — 54.9 GIH with flat IWD and ALSA 4.7 say it wheels often. A 2-drop Hamster that triggers lifegain-payoffs exists; fine in BW Bats or any white deck needing curve, never a priority.
Lifecreed Duo: Solid filler — 55.5 GIH on a dual-tribe 2-drop that attacks and gains life. Fits BW Bats (triggers lifegain) or WU Birds (flying body); fine role-player, doesn't push any archetype forward on its own.
Mabel's Mettle: Sideboard-level combat trick — 53.2 GIH and -1.1 IWD suggest it's a reactive spell that rarely creates advantage. Fine 22nd/23rd card in RW Mice where you're trying to push through; cut it for anything better.
Mouse Trapper: Below-rate uncommon — 51.8 GIH and -2.7 IWD on a 3-mana Mouse that taps blockers. The 65% play rate shows it gets included by necessity; pure solid filler, only a priority in dedicated RW Mice.
Nettle Guard: Solid filler 2-drop — 54.6 GIH on a Mouse Soldier with vigilance. Holds the ground and triggers Mouse/Soldier synergies; freely available at ALSA 5.1. Fine role-player in white decks, nothing to seek out.
Parting Gust: Solid filler interaction — 53.7 GIH on a 2-mana tempo bounce that also investigates (rarely). Better in formats with fewer ETBs to punish; passable in BW or WU where you need spell density and tempo.
Pileated Provisioner: Mediocre 5-drop Bird — 53.5 GIH and ALSA 6.2 say it wheels every time. The 39% play rate confirms this late-game filler only appears when you're desperate for a top-end body. Solid filler at best, often cut.
Rabbit Response: Solid filler combat trick — 54.7 GIH at ALSA 6.1 means it's always available late. Four mana is too much for just a pump spell; playable in GW Rabbits where Rabbit tribal upside applies, skip elsewhere.
Repel Calamity: Strong playable — 57.1 GIH and 2.7 IWD on a 2-mana instant that counters targeted removal or board wipes. White's best trick; excellent in any white deck as protection with real upside. ALSA 3.7 means it won't always be there.
Salvation Swan: Strong playable rare — 56.8 GIH and ALSA 2.0 say it's taken early. A 4-drop Bird Cleric that gains substantial life and has flying makes it a real threat in BW Bats and WU Birds; solid in any white midrange.
Season of the Burrow: Premium first-pick mythic — 61.7 GIH and 8.4 IWD on a 5-mana sorcery that creates a massive Rabbit army. ALSA 1.5 confirms it's almost always P1P1; the board presence is simply game-ending in white.
Seasoned Warrenguard: Solid filler 1-drop Rabbit — 57.1 GIH but ALSA 5.0 and 63% play rate suggest it's taken as needed. First strike makes it a real combat piece in GW Rabbits; reliable but not a priority pick in white.
Shrike Force: Solid filler — 55.5 GIH on a 3-mana flying Bird Knight that creates another Bird token. Fine in WU Birds as a mid-range threat; generic enough for any white deck, but the stats don't elevate it above filler.
Sonar Strike: Strong playable removal — 55.8 GIH on a 2-mana instant that deals 4 to attacking/blocking creatures is efficient. Best in BW Bats where the life-gain clause fires reliably; solid removal in any white deck. ALSA 4.4.
Star Charter: Solid filler — 54.1 GIH on a 4-mana Bat Cleric that draws a card when you gain life. Fine in BW Bats where it cycles itself and triggers synergies; underwhelming outside that archetype. CGB B overstates it slightly.
Starfall Invocation: Strong playable rare — 57.0 GIH and ALSA 2.1 mark a 5-mana sorcery that destroys multiple creatures. Premium removal in a removal-scarce format; take it highly in any white deck. CGB A+ is generous but it's legit.
Thistledown Players: Sideboard-level Mouse Bard — 52.4 GIH and -1.6 IWD with a 50% play rate mean it barely makes decks. Inspires other creatures when it attacks, but the payoff is too slow for what BLB decks want. Cut first.
Valley Questcaller: Premium first-pick rare — 64.1 GIH and 10.4 IWD are elite numbers. A 2-drop Rabbit Warrior that tutors Rabbits and pumps the team on attack is a game-defining engine in GW Rabbits. ALSA 1.6 confirms it — take it immediately.
Warren Elder: Solid filler — 55.5 GIH on a 2-mana Rabbit Cleric that gains life and triggers other payoffs. Fills the curve in GW or BW; freely available at ALSA 4.5. Fine role-player, never exciting.
Warren Warleader: Premium first-pick mythic — 63.2 GIH, 9.9 IWD, ALSA 1.4. A 4-mana Rabbit Knight that creates tokens and pumps the board is a game-ending bomb in GW Rabbits. Auto-first-pick; take it always.
Wax-Wane Witness: Sideboard-level Bat Cleric — 54.1 GIH at ALSA 6.0 and 49% play rate mean it barely makes cuts. A 4-mana Bat with marginal graveyard triggers; fine in deep BW Bats decks, otherwise skip.
Whiskervale Forerunner: Strong playable rare — 56.0 GIH and ALSA 2.0 mean it goes early. A 4-mana Mouse Bard that animates artifacts or grants abilities is a strong midrange threat in RW Mice; versatile enough for any white deck.
Azure Beastbinder: Strong playable rare — 59.5 GIH and 8.6 IWD on a 2-drop Rat Rogue that enslaves a creature until it dies. Tempo-positive and powerful in UB Rats where evasion payoffs stack; note CGB B- slightly undersells the GIH.
Bellowing Crier: Solid filler Frog — 53.4 GIH at ALSA 6.8 and 47% play rate means it wheels. A 2-mana 2/1 that pings when it attacks is marginal; fine as a Frog body in GU Frogs or a cheap spell-trigger in UR, never priority.
Calamitous Tide: Solid filler late-game spell — 55.9 GIH but only a 46.9% play rate and ALSA 6.0. Mass bounce at 6 mana is too slow for most BLB games; archetype-only for GU Frogs or UB control that can leverage the tempo swing.
Daring Waverider: Solid filler in UR Otters — 57.4 GIH but ALSA 5.3 and 54.5% play rate show it's available late. Green's format dominance undercut blue's top-end; still a fine 6-drop with prowess-like triggers in the right shell.
Dazzling Denial: Solid filler counterspell — 55.3 GIH at ALSA 6.6 means it wheels consistently. A 2-mana counter that cycles when you've spent mana on creatures is efficient interaction in GU Frogs or UB Rats; take a few, not a priority.
Dire Downdraft: Strong playable bounce — 56.6 GIH on a 4-mana instant that bounces a creature and untaps islands. Solid tempo swing in GU Frogs and WU Birds; the untap clause enables combat or defensive plays. Available at ALSA 6.4.
Dour Port-Mage: Strong playable rare — 58.5 GIH and 7.1 IWD on a 2-drop Frog Wizard that counters spells cheaply. Excellent tempo piece in GU Frogs and UB Rats; ALSA 2.8 means it gets picked mid-pack.
Eddymurk Crab: Solid filler late-game — 56.8 GIH on a 7-mana Elemental Crab that draws cards. ALSA 5.0 means it wheels; a serviceable top-end in GU Frogs or any blue deck that goes long, but never a priority over early-game.
Eluge, the Shoreless Sea: Archetype-only payoff mythic — 54.4 GIH and 44.1% play rate are damning for a mythic. An impressive Frog Fish that only fires in heavy-Frog GU builds; the low GIH reflects poor performance outside that shell.
Finch Formation: Sideboard-level — 51.0 GIH and ALSA 7.0 mean this always wheels. A 3-mana Bird spell that creates two 1/1 Birds is fine token filler in WU Birds; never pick it high, just grab it late if you need bodies.
Gossip's Talent: Avoid — 48.9 GIH, -1.7 IWD, and CGB D+ tell the whole story. A 2-mana Class that mills and occasionally reanimates is too slow and too narrow for a format where tempo matters. Sideboard/unplayable.
Into the Flood Maw: Strong playable — 56.5 GIH and 5.1 IWD on a 1-mana bounce that doubles as a combat trick. Expert note is key: counters green's fight-removal in real time. Premium in UB Rats; solid in any blue deck. ALSA 5.7 so it wheels.
Kitnap: Strong playable rare — 57.5 GIH and ALSA 2.8. A 4-mana Aura that takes control of a creature until it leaves is pseudo-removal that also gives you a body. Premium in GU Frogs; solid removal alternative in any blue deck.
Kitsa, Otterball Elite: Strong playable mythic — 58.5 GIH and 7.4 IWD on a 2-mana Otter that grows and generates Otter tokens. ALSA 2.5 means it's gone early; premium engine piece for UR Otters, strong in any blue midrange.
Knightfisher: Solid filler — 54.7 GIH at ALSA 5.3 and 48% play rate. A 5-mana flying Bird Knight with a relevant tap ability is a fine top-end in WU Birds; not exciting enough to pick early, grab it mid-to-late.
Lightshell Duo: Strong playable overperformer — 55.6 GIH on a card taken at ALSA 7.0 (wheels!) is remarkable. A 3/4 prowess for 4 mana that surveils 2 on ETB is excellent in UR Otters or UB Rats; grab it as a late gift.
Long River Lurker: Strong playable — 57.0 GIH and ALSA 4.4 on a 3-mana Frog Scout that loots or grows on combat. Fine in GU Frogs as both a payoff and a looter; solid role-player available mid-pack in any blue deck.
Long River's Pull: Solid filler — 54.7 GIH but 37.9% play rate and CGB C- reveal it underperforms. A 2-mana bounce that returns two creatures is clunky tempo in a format that wants efficiency; situational at best, skip outside GU Frogs.
Mind Spiral: Solid filler mill spell — 54.0 GIH at ALSA 7.3 means it's a late-pick luxury. A 5-mana mill-for-ten that gains life is fine in UB Rats grinding decks; never a priority, purely a role-player in long-game shells.
Mindwhisker: Strong playable — 57.3 GIH and 5.3 IWD on a 3-mana Rat Wizard that makes opponents discard when you connect. Excellent in UB Rats where evasion stacks; solid blue midrange piece available at ALSA 4.8.
Mockingbird: Strong playable rare — 56.7 GIH and ALSA 2.6. A 1-mana Bird Bard that copies any creature on board for X mana is an efficient mana-sink that scales into late games. Best in UR Otters or WU Birds; versatile enough for any blue deck.
Nightwhorl Hermit: Unplayable — 50.2 GIH and only 16.6% play rate confirm this Rat Rogue does nothing relevant. A 3-mana 2/2 with a weak activated ability is never correct to main-deck. Sideboard at absolute best.
Otterball Antics: Solid filler — 54.0 GIH at ALSA 5.8 and 44% play rate. A 2-mana sorcery that makes Otter tokens and grants trample is fine in UR Otters but rarely worth taking early. Wheels often; grab it late for the archetype.
Pearl of Wisdom: Solid filler — 56.4 GIH and 5.8 IWD on a 3-mana draw-2 that also scries. Better than a plain draw-2 in GU Frogs where spell density matters; fine in any blue deck at ALSA 6.8. Take it late as card advantage.
Plumecreed Escort: Strong playable — 56.3 GIH on a 2-mana Bird Scout that flies and draws a card when it attacks with the air force. Solid WU Birds engine piece; ALSA 4.4 means it's available mid-pack. Take it in blue-white.
Portent of Calamity: Archetype-only payoff rare — 52.8 GIH and 19.8% play rate say it rarely makes cuts. A 1-mana sorcery that eventually makes all your spells free is a build-around dream but a dead draw early; only for dedicated control blue. CGB A- wildly overrates it.
Run Away Together: Solid filler — 53.6 GIH at ALSA 6.9 means it always wheels. A 2-mana bounce-two is clean tempo in GU Frogs or any blue deck; bouncing your own creature to replay ETBs is a bonus. Fine role-player, not a priority.
Season of Weaving: Premium first-pick mythic — 60.7 GIH and 9.7 IWD on a 6-mana sorcery that creates multiple powerful token spells. ALSA 2.1 confirms heavy demand; a game-ending top-end for any blue deck. Take it early.
Shore Up: Solid filler trick — 54.3 GIH on a 1-mana instant that gives hexproof and +0/+1. Cheap protection spell in GU Frogs where you're fighting over your creatures vs green removal; fine role-player, never a priority.
Shoreline Looter: Strong playable — 60.4 GIH and 8.2 IWD on a can't-be-blocked 1/1 that sculpts your hand. Excellent in UB Rats and any blue deck running Builder's Talent (unblockable enabler). ALSA 3.9 means take it; it's contested.
Skyskipper Duo: Solid filler — 54.1 GIH at ALSA 6.8 and 44% play rate means it wheels. A 5-mana dual-tribe flyer is a generic top-end body in WU Birds or GU Frogs; fine filler if your curve needs a 5-drop.
Spellgyre: Solid filler — 55.9 GIH on a 4-mana instant that counters a spell and draws a card. Fine curve-filler in GU or UB decks that want interaction; the 52% play rate suggests it's slightly worse than it looks. Sideboard-adjacent.
Splash Lasher: Strong playable — 57.4 GIH and 5.4 IWD on a 4-mana Frog Wizard that grows when you cast instants/sorceries. A real threat in GU Frogs and UR Otters; available mid-pack at ALSA 4.2. Take it in spell-heavy blue decks.
Splash Portal: Solid filler — 55.2 GIH on a 1-mana sorcery that cantrips and makes a Frog token later. Clean in GU Frogs as both a cheap spell and a future token; fine in UR Otters for spell density. ALSA 5.6 means it wheels often.
Stormchaser's Talent: Strong playable rare — 58.8 GIH and ALSA 3.4 on a 1-mana Class that rewards instants/sorceries. Archetype-dependent payoff for UR Otters and GU Frogs; the GIH reflects it's only in the right decks. CGB B+ accurate — strong when built around.
Sugar Coat: Solid filler Aura — 54.9 GIH at ALSA 4.8 and 70% play rate means it's a staple filler. A 3-mana enchantment that gives a creature +2/+2 and an ability is passable in any blue deck; serviceable, unexciting.
Thought Shucker: Sideboard-level — 52.2 GIH and 23.7% play rate expose a 2-mana Rat Rogue that rarely does enough. The discard ability requires setup; fine in UB Rats as a tribal body, essentially never correct elsewhere. Wheels for good reason.
Thundertrap Trainer: Strong playable rare — 57.7 GIH and 6.1 IWD on a 2-mana Otter Wizard that punishes spells with a 3/3 body trigger. Excellent in UR Otters and GU Frogs; ALSA 3.4 means take it mid-pack in blue.
Valley Floodcaller: Strong playable rare — 55.1 GIH and ALSA 2.9 on a 3-mana Otter Wizard that bounces a creature when it ETBs and repeats every turn. Premium tempo piece in GU Frogs or UR Otters; strong signal to stay in blue.
Waterspout Warden: Sideboard-level — 52.2 GIH and only 25.4% play rate. A 3-mana Frog Soldier with a defensive ability barely makes cuts in GU Frogs; pure filler that wheels and rounds out creature counts.
Wishing Well: Unplayable outside niche — 56.6 GIH but only 10% play rate, and CGB D+. A 4-mana artifact that wishes for a card is too slow and too conditional to matter in BLB's tempo-oriented format. Sideboard/skip.
Swords to Plowshares: Premium first-pick — 58.7 GIH on the best removal spell ever printed, at ALSA 1.8. One mana, exile anything, in a removal-starved format. Auto-pick in any white deck, no discussion needed.
Ledger Shredder: Strong playable mythic — 57.9 GIH and 6.5 IWD on a 2-mana Bird Advisor that grows and draws when players cast second spells per turn. ALSA 2.3 confirms it's taken early; premium in UR Otters and WU Birds where spell density is high.
Agate-Blade Assassin: Barely-above-rate 2/1 deathtouch for 2B with a minor Lizard synergy tag. The 54.8 GIH and rock-bottom IWD confirm it as solid filler in RB Lizards or any black aggro shell, never a priority pick.
Bandit's Talent: Archetype-only payoff for UB Rats — the Talent progression rewards committing hard to the tribe, and 52.6 GIH with 4.7 ALSA signals middling demand. Strong in a Rats shell, filler outside it; don't force.
Bonebind Orator: Squirrel Warlock with a stat-line that punches above rate — 55.3 GIH and 78.9% play rate signal a reliable two-drop that slots cleanly into BG Squirrels or any black midrange build. Solid filler, pick confidently in black.
Bonecache Overseer: One-mana 2/1 with the Squirrel tribe tag and a graveyard-fueling ability that enables Forage — the 56.5 GIH and +2.4 IWD back it up as a genuine premium one-drop. Strong playable, especially in BG Squirrels where Forage is currency.
Coiling Rebirth: Five-mana reanimation rare with CGB's B grade but only 53.6 GIH — the stats and 3.4 ALSA say rares go earlier than this plays. Archetype-only payoff for grind-heavy BG Squirrels or BU value decks; don't pick it hoping the effect pays off on an average board.
Consumed by Greed: Premium black instant removal — destroy target creature at 3 mana at 58.8 GIH and +4.5 IWD, with a 3.2 ALSA confirming it's contested. Strong first-pick candidate for any black deck; removal is scarce in BLB so prioritize it.
Cruelclaw's Heist: Two-mana rare discard-and-loot with a Rats synergy hook — 53.2 GIH is uninspiring for a rare, though 3.5 ALSA shows some demand. Archetype-only payoff in UB Rats or BR control; benign filler elsewhere.
Daggerfang Duo: 3/2 deathtouch for 2B that mills 2 on ETB — solid Rat/Squirrel dual-tribe body that helps hit Forage/threshold. 55.5 GIH with 75.9% play rate confirms its role as a dependable strong playable, especially potent in UB for graveyard count.
Darkstar Augur: 60.4 GIH and a monster +6.4 IWD on a 3-mana Bat Warlock that rewards opponent life-loss — but CGB's C- diverges sharply from performance. Trust the data: premium first-pick rare, best in BW Bats or BR Lizards where life-loss triggers pile up.
Diresight: Scry 2 plus a minor bonus for 3 mana — the 54.5 GIH with only 36.1% play rate reveals drafters correctly downgrade pure selection spells. Solid filler for decks that want to set up Forage or sculpt draws, but never a high pick.
Downwind Ambusher: 4-mana Skunk Assassin that ambushes attacking creatures with a -1/-1 nerf backstory behind it — 59.6 GIH and +4.9 IWD at 3.5 ALSA make this one of black's best uncommons. Strong first-pick among uncommons; fits any black deck, punishes aggro and combat.
Early Winter: Five-mana instant removal with conditional upside — 53.5 GIH and only 48.9% play rate say it's too expensive for what it does. Solid filler at best, solid sideboard against Tokens; don't mainboard eagerly in fast games.
Feed the Cycle: Efficient 2-mana instant that kills a creature and fills the graveyard for Forage — 58.6 GIH and +4.9 IWD with a 2.7 ALSA confirm it's contested and deserves it. Premium removal for BG Squirrels and any black deck that Forages; top-tier common.
Fell: Clean 2-mana removal sorcery at 59.5 GIH and +5.9 IWD, drafted early (2.3 ALSA) and played in 91.2% of decks that see it. Premium first-pick common; black's best cheap removal, a slam in any black deck.
Glidedive Duo: 5-mana Bat/Lizard dual-tribe body bridging BW and RB — 56.6 GIH and solid play rate make this a strong playable that goes in every black deck as Lol notes. Reliable top-end filler; grabs value in Bats and Lizards arches alike.
Hazel's Nocturne: 4-mana instant with Bat synergies — 56.4 GIH is respectable but 4.7 ALSA says it wheels more than removal does. Strong playable in BW Bats, solid filler elsewhere; the instant speed is the real selling point.
Huskburster Swarm: Eight-mana Elemental Insect with a Forage/sacrifice payoff — CGB's B- and 53.7 GIH tell you it's real but costs way too much to reliably deploy. Archetype-only late game win condition for BG Squirrels grind builds; don't draft around it.
Iridescent Vinelasher: 1-mana Lizard Assassin with a combat-damage triggered ability that scales — 58.2 GIH and +4.7 IWD at 2.1 ALSA means it's heavily contested. Premium first-pick uncommon for RB Lizards; every black-red deck wants this.
Maha, Its Feathers Night: Mythic 5-mana Elemental Bird at 63.4 GIH and +9.9 IWD, taken at pick 1.3 — the format's top end of black bombs. Premium first-pick; a haymaker that warps games, fits any black deck and especially BW Bats.
Moonstone Harbinger: 3-mana 2/3 Bat Warrior with evasion and a life-gain trigger — 56.2 GIH and 4.0 ALSA show moderate demand. Strong playable in BW Bats, solid filler in any black deck; the Bat tribal tag adds archetype upside.
Nocturnal Hunger: Destroy-target-creature instant for 3 mana, gifting a Food or costing 2 life — 57.0 GIH and 90.4% play rate confirm it as premium black removal. Strong first-pick common; the instant speed and reliability make it top black common tier.
Osteomancer Adept: 2-mana 2/2 Squirrel Warlock that generates ongoing value off Forage triggers — 59.0 GIH and +4.8 IWD at 2.1 ALSA marks it as a fiercely contested rare. Premium first-pick for BG Squirrels; functions as a strong playable in any black midrange shell.
Persistent Marshstalker: 2-mana 2/2 Rat Berserker with a recursion or persistence mechanic — 56.0 GIH is solid with reliable 68.2% play rate. Strong playable in UB Rats, solid filler in any black aggro deck; not contested enough to prioritize early.
Psychic Whorl: Mill-3 sorcery at 3 mana with a 52.9 GIH but only 13.0% play rate — most drafters correctly avoid pure mill as filler. Sideboard/replacement-level only; plays in very specific self-mill or Forage threshold builds, ignore otherwise.
Ravine Raider: 1-mana 1/1 Lizard Rogue with menace — 55.3 GIH but iwd of 0.0 and only 44.8% play rate reveal it as fringe-playable aggro filler. Solid filler in RB Lizards curve, never pick it early; it wheels.
Rottenmouth Viper: 6-mana mythic Elemental Snake with a board-affecting enter trigger at 59.2 GIH and +6.6 IWD, snagged at 1.4 ALSA. Premium first-pick bomb; a game-ending top-end threat for any black deck that can reach 6 mana.
Ruthless Negotiation: 1-mana Sorcery that makes an opponent sacrifice or discard — 58.0 GIH and 5.5 ALSA shows the data supports it but drafters undervalue it. Strong playable hand disruption; best on turn 1 in BR or BU, but low ALSA means it's wheeling more than the GIH suggests.
Savor: 2-mana instant: sac a Food, destroy a creature — 59.3 GIH and +4.9 IWD confirm it as premium removal that doubles as a Forage trigger. Strong first-pick common for BG Squirrels and any black Forage shell; one of the best commons in black.
Scales of Shale: 3-mana instant that pumps a creature +2/+0 with lifelink and indestructible, cheaper per Lizard you control — 57.7 GIH and +3.2 IWD despite only 57.9% play rate. Strong playable combat trick in RB Lizards; fine one-of in any black deck.
Scavenger's Talent: 1-mana Squirrel Class enchantment that scales into a value engine — 56.3 GIH with 3.1 ALSA signals real early demand. Archetype-only payoff for BG Squirrels; the Talent tiers reward staying the course but do nothing outside the tribe. CGB C- vs actual 56 GIH is a sharp divergence — trust the data.
Season of Loss: 65.7 GIH and +13.4 IWD — the highest performer in black's entire pool, snagged at 1.6 ALSA. Premium first-pick mythic sorcery that closes games and generates overwhelming card advantage; slam it in any black deck, no conditions.
Sinister Monolith: 4-mana artifact with a 51.3 GIH and -1.1 IWD — the negative IWD is damning, meaning you actively perform worse when you draw it. Sideboard/replacement-level; only consider it for specific ramp plans or fixing in non-black builds.
Stargaze: 2-mana draw-2-lose-2 sorcery at 54.5 GIH but low 36.9% play rate — drafters rightly recognize the life-loss matters in BLB's removal-light format. Solid filler in Food/lifegain shells (BG, BW) that offset the cost; risky in RB where you bleed out. CGB C+ is fair.
Starlit Soothsayer: 3-mana 2/2 Bat Cleric with a lifegain/scry trigger at 51.6 GIH and -0.9 IWD — barely playable. Sideboard/replacement-level; only clogs your curve without strong BW Bats synergy to make the life-drain relevant.
Starscape Cleric: 2-mana Bat Cleric with a combat-relevant ETB or ability — 56.7 GIH, +2.9 IWD, and 3.9 ALSA mark it as a solid early pick. Strong playable in BW Bats, solid filler in any black deck; better than it looks at first glance.
Thornplate Intimidator: 4-mana 3/3 Rat Rogue with intimidate or evasion — 54.5 GIH and 48.5% play rate mark it as below-curve. Solid filler in UB Rats for top-end pressure; nowhere near worth prioritizing, wheels reliably.
Thought-Stalker Warlock: 60.8 GIH and +6.3 IWD on a 2/2 menace for 2B that ETB-discards the opponent — the hype was earned. Premium first-pick uncommon for any black deck; RB Lizards and UB Rats love the menace and disruption combo. One of black's best uncommons overall.
Valley Rotcaller: 2-mana 2/2 Squirrel Warlock that generates Food or tokens on Forage triggers — 58.6 GIH and +5.1 IWD at 2.0 ALSA makes it black's most-contested Squirrel rare. Premium first-pick in BG Squirrels; still a strong playable in any black midrange deck.
Wick, the Whorled Mind: 4-mana legendary Rat Warlock with a hand-disruption or card-advantage engine — CGB B+ vs 54.9 GIH flags a divergence: experts love it more than data supports. Archetype-only payoff for UB Rats with a mill/discard sub-theme; solid filler outside it despite the rare frame.
Wick's Patrol: 6-mana 4/4 Rat Warlock with a repeatable ETB-style ability — 60.3 GIH and +6.3 IWD at 3.3 ALSA confirm it's a real late-game powerhouse. Strong playable top-end in UB Rats and any grindy black deck; the GIH belies the high mana cost.
Rat Colony: No GIH data and only 18.9% play rate — a unique card that scales with copies but requires critical mass never achievable in normal draft. Sideboard/replacement-level; novelty pick only, don't waste picks chasing the combo.
Relentless Rats: No GIH data at only 1.7% play rate — the ultimate 'build around 4 copies' card that plain doesn't work in limited. Skip entirely; takes too many picks from a real deck to make it functional.
Agate Assault: 3-mana red sorcery dealing damage to a creature or player — 54.3 GIH with 78.6% play rate marks it as a workhorse. Solid filler removal in RB Lizards or any red deck; not exciting, but red's removal scarcity makes this pick-up-able.
Alania's Pathmaker: 4-mana 3/3 Otter Wizard with a spell-cast trigger — 53.8 GIH and near-0 IWD show it underdelivers. Solid filler in UR Otters, replacement-level elsewhere; the low play rate confirms it gets cut from better decks.
Artist's Talent: 46.0 GIH and -1.9 IWD on a rare — CGB's D matches reality. A build-around Talent that requires specific artifact/art-token enablers that red can't reliably assemble. Hard pass; sideboard/unplayable.
Blacksmith's Talent: Equipment-class Talent that escalates into permanent double strike — 54.8 GIH and 68.8% play rate make it a solid filler inclusion in RG Raccoons or RW Mice. Archetype-only payoff; the ceiling is absurd with Starforged Sword but the floor is modest. Fine mid-pack pick.
Blooming Blast: 2-mana instant that deals 2 damage and can be replayed or has bonus mode — 54.1 GIH and 77.5% play rate make it a reliable solid filler. Goes in any red deck; not removal you prioritize but efficient enough to run without complaint.
Brambleguard Captain: 4-mana Mouse Soldier that pumps or gives your team a bonus on attack — 53.9 GIH and near-0 IWD in a 74.4% play-rate slot suggest it's a role-player. Solid filler in RW Mice; provides tribal count and a mild anthem effect but is not a priority pick.
Brazen Collector: 2-mana 2/2 Raccoon Rogue with an on-attack triggered loot effect — 54.8 GIH and 82.8% play rate with 3.9 ALSA show real early demand. Strong playable in RG Raccoons; card filtering is scarce in red so this earns its spot as a priority pick in the archetype.
Byway Barterer: 3-mana Raccoon Rogue rare that generates card advantage when it attacks or connects — 56.9 GIH and +3.0 IWD at 2.6 ALSA mark it as a contested, genuine strong playable. First-pick quality in RG Raccoons; also solid value in any red midrange deck.
Coruscation Mage: 2-mana 2/2 Otter with Offspring that pings opponent each time you cast a spell — 54.4 GIH and +1.8 IWD, best in UR Otters where spells fire the ping repeatedly. Solid filler with archetype upside; triggers 'opponent lost life' synergies and Fountain Port Bell on the cheap.
Conduct Electricity: 5-mana instant with conditional big damage — 52.5 GIH and only 30.5% play rate say drafters correctly identify it as expensive for what it does. Solid filler as a late-game finisher; runs in UR Otters or control shells that need reach, but never a high pick.
Emberheart Challenger: 2-mana 2/2 Mouse Warrior with a power-matters or attack trigger — 58.1 GIH and +4.4 IWD at 2.3 ALSA mark it as hotly contested. Premium first-pick for RW Mice; strong playable in any red aggro shell that wants early pressure.
Festival of Embers: 48.1 GIH and a catastrophic 6.6% play rate — CGB's D+ matches the collapse. A 5-mana enchantment with win-more conditions virtually nobody plays. Hard pass; unplayable.
Flamecache Gecko: 2-mana 2/2 Lizard Warlock with a Forage or sacrifice trigger — 56.3 GIH and 74.0% play rate make this a solid playable. Strong filler in RB Lizards that also bridges Forage sub-themes; pick it mid-pack with confidence.
Dragonhawk, Fate's Tempest: 5-mana mythic legendary Bird-Dragon at 62.3 GIH and +10.1 IWD, snagged at 1.5 ALSA — the data screams bomb. Premium first-pick; a flying haymaker that dominates board states, goes into any red or bird-themed deck.
Frilled Sparkshooter: 4-mana Lizard Archer with a ping/tap ability — 52.1 GIH and -2.0 IWD at 6.7 ALSA confirm it wheels for good reason. Replacement-level filler; only include in RB Lizards when the deck is desperate for tribal count.
Harnesser of Storms: 3-mana Otter Wizard with a spell-count payoff — 50.1 GIH and -0.9 IWD at 5.8 ALSA show drafters leave it. Solid filler at best in UR Otters where it may occasionally chain value; cut it from good decks.
Heartfire Hero: 1-mana Mouse Soldier with an Offspring or combat trigger that scales — 56.7 GIH and +2.2 IWD at 3.8 ALSA mark it as a valued early drop. Strong playable in RW Mice aggro; a turn-1 play that accrues value rapidly and applies early pressure.
Hearthborn Battler: 3-mana Lizard Warlock rare — 53.6 GIH and near-0 IWD but 2.7 ALSA suggests drafters overvalue it. Archetype-only payoff for RB Lizards; performs when the tribe fires but generic stats underdeliver elsewhere. CGB B feels generous given the flat IWD.
Hired Claw: 1-mana Lizard Mercenary rare at 60.2 GIH and +5.8 IWD, picked at 2.5 ALSA — a premium 1-drop that outkicks its weight in any red deck. Premium first-pick; a 1-mana threat with scaling ability that RB Lizards desperately wants on turn 1.
Hoarder's Overflow: 2-mana Expend-4 enchantment that converts excess spending into card draw — 45.4 GIH and -6.4 IWD at 7.1 ALSA tells the general story, but it's an archetype-only payoff needing an Expend-heavy RB shell. Don't force it; only strong in dedicated high-spend decks per expert notes.
Kindlespark Duo: 3-mana Lizard/Otter dual-tribe body — 49.4 GIH and -2.0 IWD signal it underperforms. Replacement-level filler that bridges RB/UR but does it poorly; cut it from any deck with better options.
Manifold Mouse: 2-mana Mouse Soldier rare at 63.1 GIH and a staggering +10.0 IWD — the format's best red card by performance. Premium first-pick; pushes you into RW Mice aggro and functions as a generically powerful 2-drop in any red deck.
Might of the Meek: 1-mana instant pump at 52.8 GIH and -1.0 IWD — the negative IWD reveals it's a trap. Pure combat trick filler that underperforms expectations; solid filler in RW Mice token/aggro only, never prioritize.
Playful Shove: 2-mana sorcery that bounces or removes a blocker — 54.1 GIH and 59.3% play rate signal a role-player. Solid filler in RW Mice or UR Otters as pseudo-removal; useful but replaceable, take late.
Quaketusk Boar: 5-mana Elemental Boar with a trample/power-scaling effect — 56.0 GIH and +2.5 IWD at 3.6 ALSA make it a solid top-end piece. Strong playable as red's premium 5-drop threat; a clean include in any red midrange deck that reaches the late game.
Rabid Gnaw: 2-mana instant that deals damage based on board state or creature power — 58.4 GIH and +4.5 IWD at 3.3 ALSA confirm it as a premier red removal spell. Premium first-pick uncommon; flexible and efficient, goes in every red deck.
Raccoon Rallier: 2-mana Raccoon Bard with a rally-the-team trigger — 51.4 GIH and -3.5 IWD paint a bleak picture. Solid filler at best in RG Raccoons for tribal count; the IWD makes it a cut candidate from any well-built deck.
Reptilian Recruiter: 5-mana Lizard Warrior that tutors or grows your Lizard squad — 53.3 GIH and -0.7 IWD at 5.7 ALSA say drafters correctly rate it as medium. Solid filler in RB Lizards for tribal count; benign replacement-level elsewhere.
Roughshod Duo: 3-mana Mouse/Raccoon dual-tribe body — 53.2 GIH and -1.7 IWD at 5.9 ALSA say it wheels. Solid filler bridging RW and RG archetypes for tribal count but rarely exciting; 22nd-card territory.
Sazacap's Brew: 2-mana instant with a minor damage or cantrip effect — 50.8 GIH and -2.3 IWD at 7.0 ALSA means it's consistently left on the table. Replacement-level filler; only in UR Otters as the last card in.
Season of the Bold: 5-mana mythic sorcery at 53.6 GIH and only +0.9 IWD despite a 2.5 ALSA — mythic overvalued here. CGB C+ matches the performance; a middling bomb that costs too much for its payoff. Solid filler at best; take it but don't get excited.
Steampath Charger: 2-mana Lizard Warlock with a spell-cast or damage trigger — 52.2 GIH and -2.8 IWD tell you it underperforms. Solid filler in RB Lizards for tribal enablement; cut from any deck with better 2-drops available.
Stormsplitter: Mythic 4-mana Otter Wizard at a brutal 42.8 GIH and -7.6 IWD — CGB's B- massively overrates this. The data is unambiguous: this card makes your deck worse. Avoid entirely; worst-performing mythic in the set.
Sunspine Lynx: 4-mana Elemental Cat rare with a punisher or reach ability — 51.3 GIH and -2.2 IWD at 3.4 ALSA reveals drafters are paying too much. CGB B doesn't match performance; replacement-level despite the rare slot, only worth it in specific red aggro finisher roles.
Take Out the Trash: 2-mana instant dealing 3 damage, with a free loot if you control a Raccoon — 56.9 GIH and +2.8 IWD at 3.9 ALSA confirm it as red's best common. Strong first-pick common; premium removal that upgrades in RG Raccoons with free card filtering attached.
Teapot Slinger: 4-mana Raccoon Warrior with a ranged damage ability — 53.6 GIH and near-0 IWD at 4.5 ALSA mark it as filler. Solid filler in RG Raccoons as a removal-adjacent body; replaceable and never a high pick.
Valley Flamecaller: 3-mana Lizard Warlock rare with an ETB or triggered damage effect — 57.1 GIH and +3.1 IWD at 2.2 ALSA make it heavily contested. Strong playable premium rare in RB Lizards; a card that justifies committing to the archetype early.
War Squeak: 1-mana Aura with an aggressive pump effect — 48.9 GIH and -3.1 IWD at 7.3 ALSA, barely played at 18.7%. Sideboard/replacement-level; auras are risky in any format and this one underperforms even in RW Mice aggro.
Whiskerquill Scribe: 2-mana Mouse Citizen with a cantrip or loot ability — 51.8 GIH and -3.3 IWD at 6.8 ALSA say it's correctly left late. Solid filler in RW Mice for tribal count and card filtering; replacement-level outside the tribe.
Wildfire Howl: 3-mana sorcery dealing 2 to all creatures (Gift mode hits 1 first then 2) — 53.6 GIH but only 32.7% play rate hides its true role: a sideboard-style sweeper. Archetype-only payoff as a hateful tool against tokens and Rabbit decks in UR; important include when the format warrants it.
Valley Rally: 3-mana instant that pumps or rallies creatures — 51.4 GIH and -2.1 IWD at 6.1 ALSA mark it as mediocre. Replacement-level filler in RW Mice aggro; wheels late and gets cut from any deck with real options.
Kindred Charge: 6-mana mythic sorcery with no GIH data and only 24.1% play rate — an explosive Offspring doubler that requires a wide tribal board to fire. Archetype-only payoff for any creature-dense tribal deck (Mice, Squirrels); win-more at its worst, game-ending at its best. Take it late and only if you're all-in.
Bakersbane Duo: Premium first-pick common — 57.3% GIH, Food on ETB, grows every time you Expend 4. Squirrel+Raccoon typing hits two tribes, and it's the consensus top-or-second-best common in BLB. Snap it, wheel nothing.
Bark-Knuckle Boxer: Strong playable Raccoon for RG — 56.3% GIH with a solid 4.0 ALSA says it's taken early and performs. A 2-mana beater in the best color that pressures boards; fits cleanly into any green beatdown plan.
Brambleguard Veteran: Strong playable Raccoon warrior for RG — 55.9% GIH with a 3.9 ALSA. Likely a three-drop that pumps its tribe; fills the curve and earns its spot in any green midrange shell without needing a specific synergy home.
Bushy Bodyguard: Strong playable Squirrel with Offspring — 56.0% GIH and Forage-friendly. Doubles as a two-trigger Offspring setup and graveyard fuel in BG Squirrels, the #1 archetype. Slightly above-average priority in green.
Cache Grab: Premium first-pick green instant — 57.7% GIH is the highest in green commons, and a 5.4 ALSA means it wheels sometimes despite being excellent. Probably a draw-a-card + Forage trigger; a slam in any green deck.
Clifftop Lookout: Solid filler Frog Scout for GU — 53.2% GIH and near-zero IWD say it's a role-player, not a driver. Fills a curve slot and likely has evasion or scrying utility, but it's the kind of card you're happy to pick 7th, not 3rd.
Curious Forager: Strong playable Squirrel Druid — 55.6% GIH in a green Forage deck. The name screams BG Squirrel payoff; Forage synergy and a Squirrel body make it better than average in the #1 archetype. Take it when you're in BG.
Druid of the Spade: Solid filler — 54.1% GIH but a negative IWD and sub-50% play rate suggest it's only making some green decks. A mana-dork or ramp piece that's fine but not exciting; wheels freely at pick 6+.
Fecund Greenshell: Premium first-pick rare — 64.0% GIH and a massive +11.4 IWD make this a format bomb. A 5-drop Elemental Turtle that likely generates Food and refills the hand; 1.6 ALSA says the table takes it immediately. Don't pass it.
For the Common Good: Archetype-only payoff, borderline unplayable — 45.6% GIH and a brutal -4.3 IWD with only 12% play rate. The 1-mana sorcery likely requires specific synergy (tokens? go-wide?) that almost never assembles in BLB's 2-color format. Hard pass.
Galewind Moose: Strong playable bomb top-end — 57.6% GIH and a 2.8 ALSA. A 6-mana Elemental Elk with what looks like built-in reach or evasion; a solid finisher in any green midrange deck. CGB's B matches the data; first-pick caliber at uncommon.
Hazardroot Herbalist: Solid filler Rabbit Druid — 51.4% GIH and a -2.4 IWD are below average for green. A mana-fixing druid body in GW Rabbits, but the numbers say it actively dilutes your draws. Play it in Rabbits if short on options; cut it if you can.
Heaped Harvest: Solid filler Food artifact — 52.7% GIH and only 56% play rate. A 3-mana Food token provides Forage fuel and incidental lifegain but is too slow to excite; filler that pulls above replacement if you need Forage triggers in a BG build.
High Stride: Solid filler green instant — 54.4% GIH but only 31% play rate says most green decks cut it. A cheap pump or speed trick that's useful but cuttable; great sideboard tech or 22nd-card inclusion when the board is contested.
Hivespine Wolverine: Strong playable green top-end — 57.6% GIH and 3.1 ALSA says the table values it. A 5-mana Elemental with likely reach/deathtouch; solid finisher in any green deck, no specific archetype required. CGB B matches.
Honored Dreyleader: Strong playable Squirrel Warrior — 55.5% GIH with positive IWD. A BG Squirrels payoff lord that earns its place mid-pack; better in the #1 archetype than out of it, but green's strength makes it a fine pick regardless.
Hunter's Talent: Premium first-pick Talent — 61.4% GIH, +7.7 IWD, and a staggering 2.1 ALSA. Green's best Talent payoff; a build-around that comes online fast in any creature-heavy green deck. The number reflects real power, not narrow setup — take it early.
Innkeeper's Talent: Premium first-pick rare Talent — 62.7% GIH, +10.1 IWD, 1.4 ALSA. The best Talent in green; a must-first-pick card-advantage engine that rewards Food and creature synergy broadly. CGB B+ undersells it; it's A-tier in practice.
Keen-Eyed Curator: Premium first-pick rare — 58.9% GIH, 2.0 ALSA. A 2-mana Raccoon that hoses opponent's Forage and threshold while growing large itself; brutally oppressive in a format built around those mechanics. Take it over most rares.
Longstalk Brawl: Strong playable removal — 55.9% GIH, 3.9 ALSA, 87% play rate. A cheap fight spell in the best color that enables Forage payoffs; green's primary interaction tool, and worth an early pick. More contested than it looks.
Lumra, Bellow of the Woods: Premium first-pick mythic — 59.8% GIH, +7.6 IWD, 1.5 ALSA. A 6-mana Elemental Bear that almost certainly floods the board with value on ETB; the kind of green mythic that ends games. First-pick, no hesitation.
Mistbreath Elder: Strong playable rare — 58.1% GIH, +5.8 IWD, 2.4 ALSA. A 1-mana Frog Warrior in GU Frogs is already interesting; the data backs it hard. Likely triggers Forage or combat math on the cheap; grab it, especially in GU.
Overprotect: Strong playable green instant — 58.2% GIH, +4.2 IWD, 3.5 ALSA. A protection/pump spell that does double duty in combat and against removal; broadly useful in any green deck. The table knows it's good — pick it over filler.
Pawpatch Formation: Strong playable green instant — 55.7% GIH, 4.7 ALSA (wheels sometimes). Three modes: make Food, draw a card, or destroy a flyer/enchantment. The Talent-destruction mode earns it main-deck status in most green builds. Versatile glue.
Pawpatch Recruit: Premium first-pick rare — 59.6% GIH, +6.4 IWD, 1.8 ALSA. A 1-mana Rabbit Warrior in GW Rabbits that likely has scaling upside; early data says it overperforms and the table knows it. Slam pick.
Peerless Recycling: Sideboard/replacement-level — 51.3% GIH, -1.1 IWD, 41% play rate. A 2-mana green instant that cycles or recycles but doesn't pull its weight in the main deck; only justifiable when you need graveyard recovery.
Polliwallop: Strong playable green removal — 56.7% GIH, 4.1 ALSA. A 4-mana instant that fights or bounces at instant speed; a valuable interaction piece in a format where green removal is scarce. Take it in any green deck.
Rust-Shield Rampager: Solid filler Raccoon — 54.4% GIH and only 60% play rate means it's conditional. A large green body with likely reach or Expend synergy; fits RG Raccoons but is cuttable in other archetypes. Wheels around pick 5-6.
Scrapshooter: Strong playable rare — 57.6% GIH, +4.0 IWD, 2.0 ALSA. A 3-mana Raccoon Archer that pings or picks off small creatures; flexible removal stapled to a body. CGB B+ is fair; it slots into any green deck without needing Raccoon density.
Season of Gathering: Premium first-pick mythic — 60.8% GIH, +8.8 IWD, 1.6 ALSA. A 6-mana green sorcery that almost certainly mass-Forages or refills your hand and board; one of the most powerful cards in BLB. Take it, no question.
Stickytongue Sentinel: Strong playable Frog — 55.8% GIH; a 3/3 reach that bounces a permanent on ETB. The bounce targets your own stuff to replay ETB triggers (Builder's Talent loops), making it far better than it looks in GU Frogs. Underrated.
Stocking the Pantry: Sideboard/replacement-level — 49.2% GIH, -3.3 IWD, 19% play rate. A 1-mana Food-generating enchantment that needs density to matter; nearly everyone cuts it. Only in a dedicated Forage/Food engine build, never a priority pick.
Sunshower Druid: Strong playable Frog Druid — 56.3% GIH, +3.8 IWD at just 1 mana, 5.7 ALSA (wheels regularly). A cheap mana accelerator that's also a Frog body for GU; excellent fixing and Forage-trigger setup. Underrated; pick it up late.
Tender Wildguide: Premium first-pick rare — 60.9% GIH, +8.0 IWD, 1.7 ALSA. A 2-mana Possum Druid that tutors or ramps into something crucial; broadly powerful in any green deck. CGB B+ is fair; the data screams early pick.
Thornvault Forager: Premium first-pick rare — 58.8% GIH, +5.1 IWD, 2.0 ALSA. A 2-mana Squirrel Ranger that generates ongoing Forage value; elite in BG Squirrels and solid everywhere green. Take it over most uncommons.
Three Tree Rootweaver: Solid filler Mole Druid — 54.9% GIH, 4.4 ALSA, 79% play rate. A 2-mana fixing or ramp creature; useful glue for any green deck that needs to hit colors or trigger Forage. Filler that almost always makes the cut.
Three Tree Scribe: Strong playable Frog Druid — 56.8% GIH, +3.8 IWD, 4.0 ALSA. A 2-mana scry/draw engine on a Frog body in GU; excellent synergy and broad utility. CGB B matches; a reliable strong playable, particularly in GU Frogs.
Treeguard Duo: Strong playable — 57.0% GIH, 4.2 ALSA, 88% play rate. A 4-mana Frog+Rabbit dual-tribe body that bridges GU and GW; solid rate, high floor. Great filler in any green deck and a rare cross-tribe signpost.
Treetop Sentries: Solid filler Squirrel Archer — 54.7% GIH, 5.7 ALSA (late pick). A 4-mana reach body that probably pings small creatures; fine in any green deck but replaceable. Pick it up when you need a defensive top-end body.
Valley Mightcaller: Premium first-pick rare — 60.4% GIH, +6.9 IWD, 1.8 ALSA. A 1-mana Frog Warrior that calls a bigger creature or enables massive combat tricks; 92% play rate confirms it's nearly always correct. High pick in any green deck.
Wear Down: Do not play — 44.7% GIH, -4.5 IWD, 4% play rate. A sorcery that destroys artifacts or reduces power but almost never lines up in BLB's creature-focused format. CGB D is charitable; this is a bulk-rare trap. Leave it in the pack.
Alania, Divergent Storm: Archetype-only payoff for UR Otters — 49.9% GIH with barely positive IWD says it underperforms even in its home archetype. A 5-mana legend that needs nonstop spell-casting to shine; fragile and high-variance. Take it only deep in UR.
Baylen, the Haymaker: Archetype-only payoff for GW/RGW — 51.4% GIH and slightly negative IWD even in its home archetype says it's below expectation. A 3-color legend in a 2-color format has structural issues; only for committed GW Rabbit-aggro builds.
Burrowguard Mentor: Premium first-pick uncommon for GW Rabbits — 60.6% GIH, +5.7 IWD, 3.8 ALSA. A 2-mana Rabbit Soldier that likely mentors tokens or pumps the tribe; elite in the #2 archetype. Snap pick when in GW.
Camellia, the Seedmiser: Premium first-pick for BG Squirrels — 61.1% GIH, +8.6 IWD, 2.6 ALSA. A Squirrel Warlock legend that rewards hoarding or graveyard play; a true signpost for the #1 archetype. Slam in BG; read it as the BG number.
Cindering Cutthroat: Solid filler for BR Lizards — 53.4% GIH and -1.6 IWD; the number reflects a mediocre common in an average archetype. A 3-mana Lizard Assassin that does combat work but doesn't wow; take it as filler, not a priority.
Clement, the Worrywort: Strong playable for GU Frogs — 57.2% GIH, +5.2 IWD, 3.1 ALSA. A 3-mana Frog Druid legend; likely draws cards or ramps in the GU Forage shell. Read as the GU number; solid pick when you're in Frogs.
Corpseberry Cultivator: Solid filler for BG Squirrels — 53.8% GIH and basically zero IWD. A 3-mana Squirrel that puts counters on things when you Forage; incidental synergy filler that's fine in BG but rarely game-winning. Wheels in mid-packs.
Dreamdew Entrancer: Premium first-pick for GU Frogs — 65.1% GIH, +13.4 IWD, 2.8 ALSA. The highest GIH in the multicolor section; this Frog Wizard likely taps or locks down boards at instant speed while being a sizable body. GU bomb, grab it.
Finneas, Ace Archer: Strong playable for GW Rabbits — 60.1% GIH, +6.8 IWD, 2.6 ALSA. A 2-mana Rabbit Archer legend that probably pings flyers and pumps the tribe; excellent in the #2 archetype. Read as the GW number; early pick when in Rabbits.
Fireglass Mentor: Strong playable for BR Lizards — 58.0% GIH, 5.0 ALSA. A 2-mana Lizard Warlock that generates card advantage via Offspring or damage; one of the better BR uncommons. Take it early when you're in Lizards.
Gev, Scaled Scorch: Premium first-pick for BR Lizards — 61.3% GIH, +6.5 IWD, 3.0 ALSA. A 2-mana Lizard legend that likely deals damage-scaling pings; a real BR bomb. Read as the BR number; the best Lizard rare in the set.
Glarb, Calamity's Augur: Premium first-pick mythic for UBG — 61.3% GIH, +11.0 IWD, 2.8 ALSA. A 3-color Frog Wizard; reads as a GU/BG overlap bomb that rewards Forage or flash spells. CGB B+ confirms power; only play if your mana supports it.
Head of the Homestead: Strong playable for GW Rabbits — 55.9% GIH, 4.4 ALSA. A 5-mana Rabbit token-maker likened to Cloud Goat Ranger; floods the board with 1/1s and threatens immediately. Read as the GW number; one of GW's best commons.
Helga, Skittish Seer: Archetype-only payoff for GW/WUG — 54.3% GIH but only 36% play rate even in its home shells. A 3-color Frog Druid that needs a critical mass of setup; the low play rate signals it doesn't reward investment. Speculative pick, mid-pack only.
Hugs, Grisly Guardian: Premium first-pick for RG Raccoons — 58.4% GIH, +7.0 IWD, 2.7 ALSA. CGB's A grade is well-earned; a 4-mana Badger Warrior that likely fights or tramples for free is a real bomb. Read as the RG number; snap it in Raccoons.
The Infamous Cruelclaw: Archetype-only payoff for BR — 54.7% GIH despite a 2.3 ALSA (taken very early, underdelivers). A 3-mana Weasel Mercenary legend; the low GIH relative to draft position flags hype over performance. Fine in BR, not a bomb.
Junkblade Bruiser: Solid filler for RG Raccoons — 52.5% GIH and barely positive IWD with a 44% play rate. A 5-mana Raccoon Berserker that fills the top of the curve in RG but isn't exciting; cut-level filler you take late.
Kastral, the Windcrested: Strong playable for WU Birds — 56.5% GIH, +6.2 IWD, 2.9 ALSA. A 5-mana Bird Scout legend that flies and draws cards; a real WU bomb in the Birds archetype. Read as the WU number; CGB B+ is accurate.
Lilysplash Mentor: Strong playable for GU Frogs — 58.4% GIH, +6.2 IWD, 4.2 ALSA. A 4-mana Frog Druid that likely Forages and bounces cards; solid top-end in GU. Read as the GU number; a reliable pick when you're in Frogs.
Lunar Convocation: Archetype-only payoff for WB Bats — 53.4% GIH and a -0.4 IWD despite a 3.4 ALSA (taken before data warrants). A 2-mana Bat enchantment that needs the tribe to fire; disappointing even in WB. Don't prioritize it.
Mabel, Heir to Cragflame: Premium first-pick for RW Mice — 63.9% GIH, +11.6 IWD, 2.4 ALSA. The top-performing multicolor rare; a 3-mana Mouse Soldier that equips herself and attacks for massive advantage. Read as the RW number — she's the best thing in Mice.
Mind Drill Assailant: Sideboard/replacement-level for UB Rats — 52.2% GIH, 29% play rate, 6.9 ALSA (wheels). A 4-mana Rat Warlock that mills or discard-attacks; too slow and low-impact for the main deck in most builds. Filler at the bottom of a Rat deck.
Moonrise Cleric: Solid filler for WB Bats — 55.6% GIH, 4.7 ALSA. A 3-mana Bat Cleric that likely drains or gains life on Bat ETBs; serviceable tribal body with incidental value. Decent filler in WB, nothing you're excited to take.
Muerra, Trash Tactician: Strong playable for RG Raccoons — 58.0% GIH, +6.3 IWD, 3.2 ALSA. A 3-mana Raccoon Warrior legend that rewards Foraging or recycling artifacts; read as the RG number. Solid Raccoon pick when you're in the archetype.
Plumecreed Mentor: Archetype-only payoff for WU Birds — 55.8% GIH but 5.5 IWD and only 41% play rate. A Bird Mentor that wheels frequently; needs Bird density to perform. Solid filler in WU, but the low play rate says it's cut when pools improve.
Pond Prophet: Strong playable for GU Frogs — 58.0% GIH, +5.2 IWD, 4.6 ALSA. A 2-mana Frog Advisor that likely scrys or draws on attack; great value creature in GU Frogs. Read as the GU number; above-average pick in the #3 archetype.
Ral, Crackling Wit: Premium first-pick mythic for UR Otters — 59.5% GIH, +11.1 IWD, 2.2 ALSA. A 4-mana Ral planeswalker that likely cantrips and zaps; strong in UR but a genuine bomb in any blue-red pile. CGB B+ undersells a 59.5% mythic.
Seedglaive Mentor: Archetype-only payoff for RW Mice — 53.3% GIH and -1.4 IWD despite a 4.5 ALSA says it underperforms. A Mouse Soldier that mentors or equips; it's taken ahead of its ceiling in RW. Fine filler in Mice, not a priority pick.
Seedpod Squire: Solid filler for WU Birds — 52.6% GIH, 6.0 ALSA (wheels). A 4-mana Bird Scout that likely offers a card or token on ETB; low-impact filler you pick late in WU. Don't prioritize it but it fills a slot.
Starseer Mentor: Solid filler for WB Bats — 56.0% GIH, +2.9 IWD, 4.2 ALSA. A 5-mana Bat Warlock that likely scrys and gains life; a serviceable top-end curve-capper in WB Bats but unremarkable. Take it as late filler in the archetype.
Stormcatch Mentor: Archetype-only payoff for UR Otters — 52.8% GIH with positive IWD but only 36% play rate. A 2-mana Otter Wizard that mentors spells; needs spell density to justify inclusion. Filler in UR; CGB's B grade is too generous.
Tempest Angler: Solid filler for UR Otters — 51.9% GIH, 6.6 ALSA (wheels easily). A 3-mana Otter Wizard that probably pings or cantrips; replacement-level in UR when the archetype isn't coming together. Pick it 10th or later.
Tidecaller Mentor: Archetype-only payoff for UB Rats — 56.5% GIH, +5.4 IWD but only 43% play rate and 5.9 ALSA (wheels). A Rat Wizard that mentors draw or milling effects; needs Rat density. Solid when in UB Rats, but won't wheel from late position often.
Veteran Guardmouse: Solid filler for RW Mice — 52.0% GIH, -1.9 IWD, 44% play rate, 6.3 ALSA. A 4-mana Mouse Soldier that barely pulls its weight; only in a shallow Mice build as a curve-filler. Wheels freely.
Vinereap Mentor: Premium first-pick for BG Squirrels — 61.7% GIH, +7.9 IWD, 4.1 ALSA. The key Squirrel uncommon signpost; delivers real BG value and the data backs the expert note completely. Take it early in the #1 archetype.
Vren, the Relentless: Premium first-pick for UB Rats — 60.3% GIH, +10.4 IWD, 2.6 ALSA. A 4-mana Rat Rogue legend that likely generates Rat tokens or recurs threats; genuine bomb in UB Rats. Read as the UB number; take it.
Wandertale Mentor: Strong playable for RG Raccoons — 58.4% GIH, +5.3 IWD, 4.7 ALSA. A 2-mana Raccoon Bard that generates value through lore counters or card advantage; solid early in RG. One of the better uncommons in the Raccoon archetype.
Ygra, Eater of All: Premium first-pick mythic for BG — 62.8% GIH, +10.8 IWD, 1.6 ALSA. A 5-mana Elemental Cat that likely converts all creature death into life or card advantage; a dominating late-game engine. CGB A- might undersell it.
Zoraline, Cosmos Caller: Premium first-pick for WB Bats — 62.2% GIH, +9.5 IWD, 2.3 ALSA. A 3-mana Bat Cleric legend that's the clear best WB rare; likely rewards life-gain or drains aggressively. CGB A- confirmed by data. Read as the WB number.
Barkform Harvester: Sideboard/replacement-level — 52.1% GIH, -0.5 IWD, 21% play rate, 7.2 ALSA. A colorless Shapeshifter that shifts into any creature type but underdelivers; rarely worth a main-deck slot. Pick it last if you need a tribal placeholder.
Bumbleflower's Sharepot: Solid filler artifact — 52.9% GIH, 6.5 ALSA. A 2-mana artifact with modest upside (likely a Gift or food-generating effect); mostly incidental glue. Take it late if your deck wants artifact synergy or a Gift trigger but it's cuttable.
Fountainport Bell: Solid filler that overperforms in combo shells — 53.9% GIH, 6.1 ALSA. Functions like a retro Golden Egg: cantrips, triggers prowess/Coruscation effects, counts toward Forage and threshold. Run one in any deck with those synergies; otherwise cut it.
Heirloom Epic: Sideboard/replacement-level — 53.8% GIH but -1.1 IWD and only 24% play rate. A 1-mana Book artifact that likely tutors or counts as a spell; the low play rate says it rarely earns a slot. Only in dedicated artifact/spells-matter builds.
Patchwork Banner: Strong playable artifact — 59.0% GIH, +4.6 IWD, 3.1 ALSA, 90% play rate. A tribal +1/+1 anthem with an ETB trigger; mandatory in UR Otters (names Otter, turns 1/3s into 2/4s) and excellent in any go-wide tribe. Take it early.
Short Bow: Solid filler equipment — 52.6% GIH, -0.8 IWD, 5.0 ALSA. A 2-mana bow with likely reach-granting or first-strike effect; fine in decks that need a combat-math edge but never exciting. Take it late as a filler equip, not a priority.
Starforged Sword: Do not play — 48.7% GIH, -3.4 IWD, 20% play rate. CGB's D- says it all; a 4-mana equipment that costs too much to equip and underperforms even when played. Avoid in all but the most desperate decks.
Tangle Tumbler: Archetype-only payoff for GW token decks — 53.8% GIH, -0.7 IWD, only 28% play rate despite being a 6/6 Vehicle. Crew it with two Rabbit tokens (from Head of the Homestead) for a 6/6 vigilance; great in GW, mediocre elsewhere. Don't maindeck outside tokens.
Three Tree Mascot: Sideboard/replacement-level — 52.1% GIH, -0.6 IWD, 38% play rate, 6.5 ALSA. A 2-mana colorless Shapeshifter that slots into any tribe but contributes minimally; filler when you're one tribal body short. Wheels consistently.
Fabled Passage: Strong playable rare land — 56.2% GIH, +1.7 IWD, 3.0 ALSA. Premium fixing in any two-color deck; fetches basics untapped for 0 mana effectively. Never a cut — take it around pick 3-5 and improve your mana base significantly.
Fountainport: Strong playable rare land — 56.5% GIH, +3.0 IWD, 3.1 ALSA. A utility land that likely produces multiple colors or has an activated ability; near-universal play rate (86%) confirms it earns a slot in any deck. Pick it around pick 4.
Hidden Grotto: Solid filler multicolor fixer — 54.6% GIH, 5.8 ALSA. Enters tapped but produces any color; excellent as a one-of splash enabler. Pick it up late and smooth your mana when you're splashing a third color.
Lilypad Village: Solid filler Village land — 53.4% GIH, 6.7 ALSA. Enters tapped as a GU dual; the Village creature-type is a trap you'll never activate reliably. Pick it strictly for mana fixing in GU Frogs, never prioritize it.
Lupinflower Village: Solid filler Village land — 56.9% GIH, 5.7 ALSA. GW dual that enters tapped; the Village typing is a trap. Higher GIH likely reflects GW Rabbits being the #2 archetype, not the land itself. Take it late for fixing, not for the creature type.
Mudflat Village: Solid filler Village land — 57.5% GIH, 5.4 ALSA. BG dual; highest GIH among Villages, reflecting BG Squirrels dominance. Still a tapped dual you take for fixing only, not for the Village trigger. Pick it mid-to-late in BG.
Oakhollow Village: Solid filler Village land — 53.9% GIH, -1.3 IWD, 5.0 ALSA. UB dual; the Village typing is still a trap, and UB Rats is a mid-tier archetype. Take it late for fixing only; don't prioritize.
Rockface Village: Solid filler Village land — 54.3% GIH, 5.7 ALSA. BR dual for Lizard decks; still a trap for the Village creature type. Take it purely for fixing in BR Lizards; picks up around 5-6, which is appropriate.
Three Tree City: Do not play — 51.8% GIH, 4.4 ALSA, 24% play rate, CGB F. A legendary land taken earlier than it deserves; the format says it's an F and the data confirms it. Never play this; it's the defining format trap in BLB.
Uncharted Haven: Solid filler multicolor fixer — 54.0% GIH, 5.3 ALSA, 84% play rate. Enters tapped but taps for any one color; great late-pick splash enabler. Pick it up in the last few picks when you need a third color or off-color splash.
Sylvan Tutor: Archetype-only payoff for dedicated combo/ramp — no GIH data and only 29% play rate tells the story. A sorcery that tops-decks a creature in a format where creatures are your bread-and-butter; narrow and slow. Only in decks built around specific bombs.
Toski, Bearer of Secrets: Strong playable Squirrel mythic — 56.4% GIH, +3.0 IWD, 1.6 ALSA (taken almost P1P1). An indestructible 1/1 squirrel that draws a card when it deals combat damage; must-attack clause is a drawback, but free card draw is powerful. Best in BG Squirrels.
Frogmite: Sideboard/replacement-level — no GIH data, 0.3% play rate says nearly no one plays it. A retro 4-mana 2/2 that reduces cost with artifacts; BLB has almost no artifact density to make this free. Essentially unplayable.
Sword of Fire and Ice: Premium first-pick mythic equipment — 65.2% GIH, +12.7 IWD, 1.1 ALSA, 99% play rate. The highest-performing card by GIH in the entire input. A game-ending Equipment staple that wins in any deck; absolute P1P1 if you open it.
Secluded Courtyard: Solid filler tribal land — no GIH data, 38% play rate, 4.0 ALSA. Taps for colorless or any color for your chosen creature type; free fixing in a mono-tribe deck. Take it only if you're deeply committed to a single tribe and need mana consistency.
